problem_summary: 2021 MCM Problem C (data type), team 2107815. The task: using the WSDA public-sighting dataset on Vespa mandarinia (Asian giant hornet) in Washington State, build a model to (1) describe the pest's spread over time and assess precision, (2) predict the likelihood that a sighting report is a mistaken classification, (3) use that to prioritize which reports to investigate, (4) define a method and frequency to update the model as new reports arrive, and (5) propose evidence that the pest has been eradicated. The dataset is extreme-imbalance: only ~14 positive-IDs vs thousands of negative-IDs, with three data modalities (spatio-temporal coordinates+date, free-text Notes, and report images). The team's central idea is a tri-modal fusion classifier whose three branches each emit a confidence, combined by a weighted sum, plus a forgetting-factor incremental update scheme and a three-condition eradication test.
models_used: Logistic growth + diffusion model for hornet colony spread (piecewise: linear birth-death below K/2, logistic with a 1/(1+e^{-rt}) sigmoid factor at or above K/2)
Seasonal reproduction-rate switching: alpha defined as a 6-branch piecewise function by month (0 in Nov-Mar, distinct rates in breeding peaks Apr-May and Aug-Sep) to encode hornet biology
Colony migration rule: when density exceeds threshold K the queen takes ~half the colony and migrates up to a 30 km radius (N_{i+1}=N_i/2 - mu*N_i)
Gaussian (normal) distribution to model the randomness/human noise in WHERE and WHEN a sighting is reported vs the true nest location; coordinate jitter (x_{i+1}=x_i + p(x_i)*dx)
Distance-based spatio-temporal confidence function: piecewise quadratic-then-reciprocal decay y(d) keyed to d_max+30km, giving P_SpaceTime

LDA (Latent Dirichlet Allocation) topic model on the Notes free-text, with bag-of-words, multinomial-Dirichlet conjugacy, expectation estimate of word probabilities, solved by Gibbs sampling, to give P_Text
6-layer CNN (input -> conv/max-pool stack -> dense -> softmax) for bee-vs-wasp image classification with softmax cross-entropy loss, to give P_Image
Tri-modal weighted-sum fusion: P_classification = w1*P_SpaceTime + w2*P_Text + w3*P_Image, weights set by the amount of effective information each modality carries per report

Forgetting-factor incremental update model: lambda = 0 if S_new<=S_last else 1-exp(-(1-S_last/S_new)^2); decays old LDA/CNN training data (D' = D*(1-lambda)+D_neg) and triggers retraining only when a new positive-ID lies farther than the current frontier
Eradication evaluation rule: a 3-condition test using R_now (frontier radius) frozen over a time threshold T=3*growth_cycle=75 days during the May-Oct active season with zero new positive-IDs
DEM/contour (high-line) terrain analysis of Washington State to argue habitat suitability (<3000m elevation)
key_techniques: Multi-modal data decomposition: explicitly splitting each report into three orthogonal 'dimensions' (space-time / text / image) and modeling each separately before fusing
Confidence-as-output design: every branch and the fused model emit a calibrated probability/confidence, not just a hard label - which is exactly what Q3 (prioritization) needs
Handling extreme class imbalance for the CNN by importing an external labeled Kaggle 'bee vs wasp' dataset (4943 pos / 3183 neg) for train+validation, using the contest data only as the test set

Domain-knowledge-driven parameterization: encoding hornet life cycle (wintering, two breeding peaks, 30km flight/migration range, 25-day egg-to-adult cycle) directly into model coefficients and thresholds
Modeling reports as noisy observations of a latent process via a Gaussian, separating 'eyewitness event distribution' from 'true population distribution' - a sophisticated, honest framing
Physically-grounded accuracy check: verifying the diffusion model by confirming simulated mean d_max (20.96 km) < the known 30 km biological flight radius
Frontier-radius (R_now) as a single scalar summary of invasion extent, reused across the update trigger AND the eradication criterion
Closing the loop on Q4/Q5 with a self-correcting update and a falsifiable, time-bounded eradication test
Validation discipline: confusion matrix for LDA, train/val/test loss+accuracy curves for CNN, goodness-of-fit reported separately for positive-ID and negative-ID
why_it_won: "It answered all five sub-questions with a single coherent architecture rather than five disconnected mini-models, and it did so with genuine machine learning (LDA + CNN) layered on top of a biology-grounded diffusion model - a level of ambition and data utilization that stands out in Problem C. Judges reward teams that USE all the provided data; this team is one of the few to systematically exploit the images (via CNN) and the free-text Notes (via LDA), not just the coordinates. The fusion design directly produces a per-report confidence, which maps cleanly onto the prioritization ask (Q3) and the eradication ask (Q5), so the model is purposeful rather than decorative. It also confronted the dataset's hardest, most distinctive feature head-on - the brutal ~0.5% positive rate / 14-vs-3000+ imbalance - and solved it pragmatically by sourcing an external balanced training set. The forgetting-factor update gave a quantified before/after improvement (~10% lift in negative-ID goodness-of-fit), and the eradication test is concrete and falsifiable (3 conditions, 75-day window). Reported fits (92.86%/85.22%, rising to ~95%/92% after update) plus a clean two-page memo with an actionable six-level (S-E) triage system gave the judges a complete, decision-ready package."
section_highlights: "Strong structure: a 5-question restatement up front that the whole paper then answers in order, so a judge can map every section to a deliverable. The 'Our Approach' bullet list and a tri-modal architecture flowchart (Fig 5-6) give an early mental model. Each sub-model follows the same disciplined template - establishment (with derived equations), then validation (confusion matrix / loss curves / scatter overlays), then an honest admission that the single modality is insufficient, which motivates the fusion - a persuasive narrative arc that makes the comprehensive model feel earned rather than arbitrary. The seasonal/biology trend analysis (Section 3.3) is woven in early and then reused to justify model coefficients later, giving continuity. Notation table, explicit assumptions with justifications, separate positive-ID vs negative-ID metrics throughout, and a clean Strengths/Weaknesses section. The memo translates the math into a six-level S-E priority triage and three eradication conditions, with the vivid 'only ~0.5% of reports are correct' framing to motivate resource triage - exactly the right register for the Department of Agriculture audience."
pitfalls_or_limitations: The diffusion/growth model's many parameters (alpha_1..alpha_5, K, r, mu, sigma) are never actually fit or numerically reported - they are described but not calibrated to data, so the spatio-temporal branch is closer to a plausible simulation than a fitted model

Several equations are internally inconsistent or sloppy: the alpha piecewise function lists 'Jun-Jul' twice, the confidence function (Eq 4-7) mixes variables d and x, and Eq 4-2's logistic form is non-standard - a careful judge would notice these

The fusion weights w1,w2,w3 are asserted to depend on 'amount of effective information per dimension' but the actual weighting rule/values are never specified, making P_classification non-reproducible
Heavy reliance on low-quality sources: references are mostly Baidu Baike, Zhihu, and CSDN blog posts rather than primary literature; only one real journal citation (Zhou 1996) and one Kaggle dataset

'Goodness of fit' numbers (92.86%, 85.22%, etc.) are reported without defining the metric or showing test-set sizes; the LDA branch alone had <50% positive accuracy (confusion matrix 0.46), so the headline fusion numbers are weakly substantiated

Using an external Kaggle bee-vs-wasp dataset to train the CNN risks domain shift - those images differ from real low-quality public phone snapshots, and the 'test' on contest data conflates two distributions

Tiny positive-ID sample (n~14) means every reported accuracy has huge variance; conclusions about spatial concentration rest on a handful of points, partly acknowledged but not quantified (no confidence intervals)

OCR/translation artifacts and embedded watermark text ('微信号', '关注数学模型') reveal the paper was lifted from a Chinese modeling-prep pipeline, and some figures (e.g. Fig 7-3 with sign-flipped longitudes) are visibly corrupted
Memo date is 'February 8, 2020' - before most of the 2020 data the model uses - an internal timeline inconsistency
reusable_patterns: Multi-modal fusion blueprint for any Problem C with mixed data: split each record into modality branches (numeric/spatio-temporal, text, image), build a confidence-emitting model per branch, fuse with an information-weighted sum - directly transferable to other 'noisy report' datasets

Confidence-to-priority pipeline: turn a probabilistic classifier output into an operational triage system (here S-E six levels by confidence band) to answer 'how should the client allocate limited resources' - a recurring Problem C ask

External-data rescue for class imbalance: when the contest's positive class is tiny, train on a balanced public dataset (Kaggle) and reserve the contest data as the test set - cite the source and acknowledge domain shift

Reports-as-noisy-observations framing: explicitly distinguish the latent true process from the observed/reported events and bridge them with a Gaussian noise model - a principled way to handle crowdsourced/citizen-science data

Biology/physics sanity check on a learned or simulated model: validate a spatial model by confirming an emergent quantity (mean spread distance 20.96km) stays under a known physical bound (30km flight range)

Forgetting-factor incremental update: a lightweight, justifiable answer to 'how do you update the model over time and how often' - trigger retraining only when a new event extends the frontier (S_new > S_last), decay old data by lambda

Falsifiable eradication/termination criterion: define a scalar frontier metric (R_now) and declare the phenomenon ended only if it stays frozen with zero new positives over a biologically-derived time window (3x life cycle) during the active season

Per-class metric reporting: always report goodness-of-fit separately for the rare positive class and the common negative class so a single high aggregate number can't hide poor minority-class performance

Same-template sub-model writing (Establishment -> Validation -> 'single modality insufficient' -> motivates fusion) as a persuasive structural device that makes a complex combined model feel inevitable
